title: daemon/relay.rs: PeerGroupUpdate and PeerRetract skip authorization when state.store() is None
description: In daemon/src/relay.rs, both PeerGroupUpdate (lines 350-362) and PeerRetract (lines 319-329) use the pattern: if let Some(store) = state.store() { check ownership } else { true }. When state.store() is None, all PeerGroupUpdate and PeerRetract messages are authorized unconditionally. Any MLS peer can then add/remove/promote/demote members or retract any message. Current production always passes Some(jmap_store), but the else { true } branch creates a footgun for future storeless configurations and is structurally wrong — missing authorization must default to deny, not allow.
